entry.title: Re: Goods movement not possible with mvmt type 704
entry.content: Check SAP note 2304535

entry.title: Re: In S4 HANA Public Cloud where do I find Configuration for OKYD and OKEQ?
entry.content:
Hi Dan

Looks like you might be on a Solution Builder system versus a CBC -based configuration. 

Could be some of the naming is different, not sure.

You could expand your search to 'Cost' or 'Costing'.

You can find a list of all the available SSCUIs (configuration apps) on the Process Navigator, which might help

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest

entry.title: how shipping pt. get capture in OBD via transfer posting
entry.content:
Hello Team,

we are following the process of transferring the material from EWM sloc to IM sloc via transfer posting so OBD getting created for that, and now we want know from which config shipping point get captures.

in regular OBD, created against SO, STO, subcon PO shipping pt will get captures through shipping point determination config (based on loading group, shipping condition and plant) but unable to find shipping pt determination for OBD created via transfer posting.

can anyone let me know how it get capture.

entry.title: Supplier UX Study
entry.content:
Hi all! We are looking for a few suppliers willing to participate in a brief user experience survey regarding the in-app messaging you see upon logging into your SAP Business Network account.

We are requesting ~30 minutes of your time to better understand your preferences around visual cues, copy lengths, and other factors that would help you consume relevant content easier. We are looking to conduct these conversations over the next few weeks.

You qualify for this study if:

  • You have a standard or enterprise account
  • You have logged into your SAP Business Network account in the last month (after March 16th)

Are you interested in participating? If so, please reach out to us at supplierspotlightprogram@sap.com to let us know.

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting - Asset Revaluation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release
entry.content:
Hi Leonardo. 

Try to SE38 and J_1AAINFL_REVERSE. 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Perform SAP Principal Propagation with Microsoft Entra ID for SAP SuccessFactors!
entry.content:
Hi @Martin-Pankraz  ,

Thank you for this informative blog!

We have a requirement where an external system needs to access the SAP SuccessFactors OData API. We plan to use SAP API Management and SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) for this purpose.

We have already set up IAS as a proxy to Microsoft Entra ID IDP. How should the subsequent configuration be done in SAP API Management to enable Principal Propagation to SAP SuccessFactors?

 

BR

Fethi

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content:
I just had a breakthrough: if you just add the line "conn.setencoding(encoding='iso-8859-1')", it appears that the "LONGBINARY" type issue disappears, and the query executes near-instantly. I'm investigating further now, I'll edit this comment once I know more

EDIT: I'm also going to add this as a TL;DR, but this code now works for me:

conn = pyodbc.connect(
"DRIVER={};SERVER={};UID={};PWD={};PORT=5000;APP=SelectTest;".format(
db_config["driver"],
db_config["server"],
db_config["uid"],
db_config["pwd"],
),
autocommit=True,
)
conn.setencoding(encoding='iso-8859-1') # this is the most important line!
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute("select * from files where filename = ?", [filename_to_query])

print(cursor.fetchall())

entry.title: Re: RAP Application with two Root View Entity with Associ...
entry.content:
Create should not be implemented in child entity on behavior definition, Below link has detailed example, please do a comparison to see what you have missed, in case you still have the issue, please share your behavior definition code.

entry.title: Re: RAP Application with two Root View Entity with Associ...
Re: how shipping pt. get capture in OBD via transfer posting
entry.content: Through which transaction, you were doing transfer posting?

entry.title: Re: RAP Can't read entities with %tky enabling draft
entry.content:
Hello,

in line 10 you are actually reading "bookings", not "travels". Therefore you could add "BookingID" to the field list of line 8. 
In line 18 the %tky of entity Travel is expected. You can place the cursor on it and press F2. However, travel-%tky, which is actually booking-%tky, is not compatible.

I don't know why it would work w/o draft.

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Editing custom hierarchy in SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a custom hierarchy directly in my SAC story (not at model level) for GL Account Dimension. I can see that hierarchy and use it in my story. Now I want to edit this hierarchy but I do not see any edit options other than creating a new custom group. Any idea how to edit existing hierarchy.

 

Regards

Jitendra

entry.title: Re: Achieving cost recovery standards in EPPM
entry.content: It is not possible to create new Rev Rec Keys in S/4HANA Public cloud.  I am checking if there are BADIs available.  The Cost Recovery rev rec method seems more related to direct sales and not project work.

entry.title: SAP Signavio: year 3 as a Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ Leader
entry.content:
They say good things come in threes! For the third year in a row, SAP Signavio has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for process mining platforms.

As per Gartner, Leaders execute well against their current vision and are well positioned for tomorrow. For process mining platforms, Gartner assessed sixteen vendors and, for the third year running, named SAP Signavio as a Leader, based on our ability to execute and the “completeness” of our vision.

To download a copy of the Gartner  Magic Quadrant for process mining, click the chart below:

 

A wide-angle view of a specific market

Gartner Magic Quadrant is a culmination of research in a specific market, giving you a wide-angle view of the relative positions of the market’s competitors. The positioning of the vendors in the process mining field is the culmination of Gartner’s research into that market and its players.

By applying a graphical treatment and a uniform set of evaluation criteria, a Magic Quadrant helps you quickly ascertain how well technology providers are executing their stated visions and how well they are performing against the Gartner market view.

Solutions for business process management

SAP Signavio was founded in 2009 to provide enterprise with business process management solutions to help our customers understand how their business currently works and how processes can be improved. Process mining is a key part of this endeavor, allowing organizations to drill deep down into their operations and understand where processes are working and where they aren’t, down to the level of a single invoice.

In 2021, Signavio was acquired by SAP, as a crucial tool for driving business transformation for SAP customers. Since then, we’ve continued to provide business process management solutions for organizations that use SAP and those that don’t.

Why we’re a  Leader

We believe that Gartner recognized SAP Signavio as a Leader in its Magic Quadrant for process mining for the five key aspects to SAP Signavio that are unique:

1. Customer value

Our priority has always been providing the greatest-possible value to our customers. We believe that process mining is key to success in business process management, so that’s a key capability of our solution.

2. Enterprise observability

SAP Signavio customers gain full visibility of their entire organization and the processes that it’s made up of. This allows them to centralize and manage their transformation initiatives from a single solution.

3. AI innovation

Artificial intelligence (AI) technology is a revolution for enterprise, and business process management  and process mining are no different. SAP Signavio includes custom-built AI innovations that empower you accelerate time to insights and democratize process mining, lowering the barriers of entry to this expert field.

4. Value accelerators

Value accelerators are best-practice templates and guidance that can be downloaded directly into our solutions to give you answers to your most important questions. It’s like having your own business process management consultant helping you make the most of what you’re trying to achieve with SAP Signavio.

5. User experience

SAP Signavio bridges the gap between your operations on paper and the real-world experience of your employees, customers and suppliers working with your processes. This allows you to see the true pain points and opportunities for improvement.

Empowering our customers to transform

SAP Signavio solutions continue to drive real value for our customers. In our opinion, our third inclusion on the Gartner Magic Quadrant for process mining again shows that we’re delivering on our vision for business process management. As Dee Houchen, Chief Marketing Officer at SAP Signavio, explained:

“Our goal is to empower our customers to build transformation as an ongoing capability, rather than a one-off project. As transformation never ends, our solutions help companies secure business success as they continuously adapt and analyze their operations, receive AI-assisted improvement recommendations, and monitor their business to establish continuous process optimization.

“Process mining is at the heart of this approach, and we feel being recognized as a Leader for the third consecutive year by Gartner re-affirms our unwavering commitment."

In today’s dynamic business landscape, process mining is vital for gaining data-driven insights that boost efficiency, smarter decision-making and business growth. The Gartner Magic Quadrant equips businesses with valuable insights to make informed decisions on their process mining solution.

Download here a complimentary copy of this year’s Gartner Magic Quadrant for process mining from SAP Signavio

 

Gartner disclaimer:

Gartner does not endorse any vendor, product or service depicted in its research publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner research publications consist of the opinions of Gartner's research organization and should not be construed as statements of fact.

Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this research, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.

GARTNER is a registered trademark and service mark of Gartner and Magic Quadrant is a registered trademark of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates in the U.S. and internationally and are used herein with permission. All rights reserved.

entry.title: Bubble text window not displaying properly
entry.content:
The text editor for the practice and explanation bubbles has suddenly not been displaying properly. The buttons allowing me to view the source code and the OK and Cancel buttons get cut-off and sometimes the editor itself is completely hidden. While not a showstopper, it is somewhat of a pain to always have to stretch out the editor to be able to view the buttons.

Anyone else experience something similar?

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manaer 7.2 SP15 - Retrofit configuration
entry.content: Hi SWU, Thank you for the link shared. The retrofit is between S4 HANA systems.

entry.title: Read Lock strategy to consider while moving sftp / file adapter to BTP Cloud Integration.
entry.content:
Read Lock strategy to consider while moving sftp / file adapter with – “Msecs to wait before Modification check” while migrating to BTP – Cloud Integration.

SAP Process Integration (PI) offers a "File Modification Check" that enables adapters to delay file processing for a defined period, ensuring only complete files are handled. A comparable feature in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Cloud Integration would significantly enhance file-based integration. While many file transfer scenarios necessitate source system handling to guarantee fully written files before processing, the SFTP adapter currently lacks a built-in option to specify such a waiting period.

SAP BTP Cloud Integration offers several options for implementing a read lock strategy with the SFTP adapter

 

These Includes:

Done File Expected: This strategy requires a "done file" to signal when a file is ready for processing. If this option is selected, the name of the .Done file must be provided. The .done file serves as a signal that the file is ready for processing and must be located in the same folder as the file to be processed.

 

Content Change: Monitors changes in the file length/modification timestamp to determine if the write operation on the file is complete and the file is ready to be read. With this option selected, the system waits for at least one second until there are no more file changes.

While this method can detect some changes, it is unreliable when the file is updated slowly, as the system may miss changes if they don’t occur within the fixed time. This limitation makes it unsuitable for scenarios where files are being written gradually or slowly.

Rename: This strategy renames the file on the SFTP server before reading it.

None: This picks up the file as soon as it pools. There is a lot of chance that a incomplete or half-baked file might be picked up.

 

Now to avoid picking up of half-baked / incomplete file in PI/PO (7.5) file adapter there was below option:

 

 

However, with ‘Content Change’ Read lock strategy option of BTP – CI Sftp adapter possess various disadvantages

  1. Unlike PI you can’t fix how much time to wait, and it normally waits for a second, which can result in incomplete files being picked in case of large files written via slow network.
  2. It also doesn’t pick zero length file, if that is required due to business requirement.

See - https://me.sap.com/notes/3347842/E

 

Alternatives to consider:

  1. Write done file: if the sender application can write any .done or any other Done file. The important point to remember is the done file will be deleted on successful completion of iflow.
  2. Timing the sftp adapter to pick file in certain time slots which is different from time slot used by source to write file. Interval in Time slot between source writing a file and Sftp adapter picking the file will mostly ensure no incomplete files are picked.

There might be other suitable alternatives which has not been discussed here. Kindly comment below.

Here is another fine blog which explains things in detail:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/read-lock-strategy-and-post-processing-options-in-sftp-sender-adapter-of/ba-p/13552916

entry.title: Re: Foundation Object behavior in Story Report after 1H_2...
entry.content: Subbu - Thank you for the response. I am the implementation partner, so I followed the steps that you provided. Unfortunately, this caused the installed standard reports to stop working completely. I decided to reenable the FO Translation feature and run the job again. This restored the reports, and it seems to have also fixed the initial issues that we were experiencing with the older FO objects. It would be great if this item was logged under the KBA for 1H_2025 Issues (https://me.sap.com/notes/2692971/E). As of today, there is nothing showing up for Analytics in this KBA at all.

entry.title: Joule in SAP Build
entry.content: Hi, I've been having trouble with Joule in SAP Build. It's unresponsive and I'm getting the message "Could not generate plan," regardless of the instructions I give it. Could someone help me figure out what I should do or why I'm getting this error?

entry.title: SAC – Integrated Financial Planning – DRIVER6 & allocation weights
entry.content:
Hello SAP-Community,

In the sample End-to-End Planning Process in Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA we find the process step “Plan and Allocate Cost Center Expenses” 

Here we can maintain cost center allocation weights. However, when working with my imported data, I get no results.

Upon inspection of the data, this seems to be because the transactional data (facts) in the model SAP_FI_IFP_IM_OPEX never have any value for the measure DRIVER6 nor for any similar measures (e.g. DRIVER1, DRIVER2 etc...)

 

My questions to you are the following:

1. Are these drivers defined in the source system (S4HANA Public Cloud) or somewhere in SAC? If in SAC, where can we define them?

2. Why are these values all null right now?

3. If an import is successfully performed, should all the drivers have defined values or can there be (null) values?

entry.title: Cannot log into SAP Business Application Studio Trial Edition
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to access the SAP Business Application Studio from the BTP Cockpit trial edition.  I have an S-ID with a subscription to the SAP Learning Hub.  I have my SAP BTP Trial account set up.  I have my trial subaccount set up.  The Cloud Foundry Environment and Kyma Environment are up and running.  I have a dev space created in the Cloud Foundry.

When I go the the Trial Home of the BTP Cockpit, I click on SAP Business Application Studio.  It asks me for a username and password, so I enter the one that I'm currently logged into the cockpit with and I get an error message.  This is what I get:

I'm not sure what I am doing wrong to get this message.  When I go into the subaccount I created, I go to Security -> Users and I have added the following roles (I found a post stating these role collections should be added to my account):
- Business_Application_Studio_Administrator
- Business_Application_Studio_Developer
- Business_Application_Studio_Extension_Deployer

Has anyone run into this problem or have any idea as to what I need to change?  The error message also gives me a troubleshooting correlation ID but if you go to create a message to send to SAP through me.sap.com, it tells you they don't support messages for free trials of products.

Thanks in advance,
Kevin

entry.title: Re: Implementing direct links to dashboard activities via...
entry.content:
Hi

Deep link format for FSM web:

Activity:

https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/planning-dispatching/activities/view/${activityId}/details?selectedCompanyId=${companyId}

eg: 

https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/planning-dispatching/activities/view/2FC3139E1DBA4E8B99D0C06AA8BA7D18/details?selectedCompanyId=36837

 

Service-call:

https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/planning-dispatching/service-calls/view/${servicecallId}/details?selectedCompanyId=${companyId}

eg: 

https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/planning-dispatching/service-calls/view/1BCE3236CBBD403490668CA6B96077D7/details?selectedCompanyId=36837

 

Emails : 

Emails support html components. So, you can just use an Anchor tag like below :

<a title="Activity Link href="https://us.fsm.cloud.sap/shell/#/planning-dispatching/activities/view/${fsmEvent.activityId}/details?selectedCompanyId=36837"><strong>Activity Link</strong> </a>

These direct links would open in FSM like this : 

 

Web Notifications:

To my knowledge, web notification are simple text based. You can send the entire above URL as text but cannot create an hyperlink.

Mobile Push notifications/Deep links: 

Please check help doc for more details

entry.title: Re: How to get configurable Material data of PR in RFQ fo...
entry.content:
Hi Ravi_Solanki,

Could you let us know what fields are missing and what data you want to add to the RFQ?

(Is the Sales Order output an option?)

Taking a look at the data source, the FDP does not seem extendable for additional standard fields. (Datasource Extensions app)

That means the options to investigate would be either adding Custom Fields, or if the output supports Attachments, that could be an option. For example, PO supports adding the attachment to the output. I have not tested this for RFQs.

You can investigate developer extensibility to see what you can do for RFQ:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/259a396e6bdb4d08b130049880a3920f.html?locale=en-US

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_REQUESTFORQUOTATIONTP

Developer extensibility has some ability to Print Forms, but you need to check if it is an option for RFQ:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/959664fed2114b7ba070c5bc8b4beba2.html?locale=en-US

If neither of those are options work, you could look into a side-by-side extensibility similar to 

https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/2401

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: Re: how shipping pt. get capture in OBD via transfer posting
entry.content: i'm using MIGO transaction, transfer posting against other.

entry.title: Using Routine in "Delete overlapping request" variant of Process chain in SAP Business warehouse
entry.content:
We had a target ADSO in B4HANA and there are 2 DTP's loading it, one DTP runs on all dates except 1st date of the month and other DTP runs only on the 1st date of the month.

We had 2 scenarios here which are given below, lets name the DTP’s that runs all dates except 1st as "DTP A" and DTP runs only on 1st as "DTP B".

  • DTP B when run on 1st must delete the data loaded by DTP A.
  • DTP B when run on 1st must not delete the previous loads from DTP B

To get this scenario, we can use "Delete overlapping request" in the process chain and we need to use the ABAP routine. This blog details on the same.

The routine where the ABAP code will be written in highlighted inside the variant.

We have a routine getting generated with same name as the variant name “compute_<variant name>”.

We have a global area to define the global variable and internal tables.

There are few "using" and "changing" parameters

I_REQUEST_JUST_LOADED – The request that is loading the data

I_DTP – Table containing dtp name, target adso, source object

C_T_REQUESTS_FOR_DEL - contains all active requests of the target ADSO, that can be deleted or                                                               can be removed from deletion. This is an internal table containing 2                                                                     columns,  “request” and “will_be_deleted” are the columns. “request” is the                                                         request id and  "will_be_deleted" can be set to ‘X’ if needs to be deleted.

Code

 field-symbols <l_request> type cl_rsdso_olrproc=>request_type.
  clear e_subrc.


" To get DTP details and this can be used when multiple DTP's are used
" to the target
  select process_tsn, process_id
  from rspmprocess into table (lt_idnti)
  for all entries in
  @c_t_requests_for_del
  where
  process_tsn = @c_t_requests_for_del-request.

  loop at lt_idnti into data(la_idnti).
   write: / 'Request ID ', la_idnti-process_tsn,
            'DTP used to Load ', la_idnti-process_id.
  endloop.


  loop at c_t_requests_for_del assigning <l_request>.

    read table lt_idnti into la_idnti with key
    process_tsn = <l_request>-request.

    "When we have data loaded on 1st,
    "we check if there are any previous data loads from
    "DTP_8IHQ1NO4FEU72L924FCHGP5EQ,
    "if it is present, then we need to delete the request.
    if sy-subrc = 0 and
    la_idnti-process_id = 'DTP_8IHQ1NO4FEU72L924FCHGP5EQ'.
     <L_REQUEST>-WILL_BE_DELETED = 'X'.
    else.
     <L_REQUEST>-WILL_BE_DELETED = ''.
    endif.

    write : / 'Incoming Data ',
              'Request ID ', <l_request>-request,
              'DTP Loaded it ',la_idnti-process_id,
              'will_be_deleted?(X Means "YES") ',
              <l_request>-will_be_deleted.

  endloop.

Code is detailed below,

The below code will help us to identify the request id in the target ADSO and DTP from where the request id got loaded.

This is needed for us, as we need to identify the requests from DTP A and we need to delete it.

  select process_tsn, process_id
  from rspmprocess into table @DATA(lt_idnti)
  for all entries in
  @c_t_requests_for_del
  where
  process_tsn = @c_t_requests_for_del-request.

The below statement will generate the “spool list”  when the process chain run in the background.

This is just a way to investigate on what really happened or help us to backtrack.

We can omit it, but I included just to backtrack.

  loop at lt_idnti into data(la_idnti).
   write: / 'Request ID ', la_idnti-process_tsn,
            'DTP used to Load ', la_idnti-process_id.
  endloop.

The internal table C_T_REQUESTS_FOR_DEL is looped and we pass the request id to the previous internal table LT_IDNTI. This is needed to get the DTP.

When we have data loaded on 1st we check if there are any previous data loads from DTP A if it is present, then we  delete the request by setting WILL_BE_DELETED = 'X'
Again write statements here are just to generate the spool list and help us back track

  loop at c_t_requests_for_del assigning <l_request>.

    read table lt_idnti into la_idnti with key
    process_tsn = <l_request>-request.

    "When we have data loaded on 1st,
    "we check if there are any previous data loads from
    "DTP_8IHQ1NO4FEU72L924FCHGP5EQ,
    "if it is present, then we need to delete the request.
    if sy-subrc = 0 and
    la_idnti-process_id <> 'DTP_8IHQ1NO4FEU72L924FCHGP5EQ'.
     <L_REQUEST>-WILL_BE_DELETED = 'X'.
    else.
     <L_REQUEST>-WILL_BE_DELETED = ''.
    endif.

    write : / 'Incoming Data ',
              'Request ID ', <l_request>-request,
              'DTP Loaded it ',la_idnti-process_id,
              'will_be_deleted?(X Means "YES") ',
              <l_request>-will_be_deleted.
  endloop.

Process chain is designed to add the decision between alternative variant to run the data load based on condition 1st and non 1st

Inside the “Decide Between Multiple Alternatives”

entry.title: DTIS Table not getting updated
entry.content:
We recently transported the 0FI_GL_14 extractor with a few modifications, which resulted in the associated transformations becoming inactive.

The transport error message is as follows:
DTIS table "/BIC/BD000000061" cannot be updated. Possibly due to an incompatible change.
Object reference: 0CXHLJX9AN7J5LNMPCVVAOQP21J8CJ58

We attempted a cleanup using transaction RSDTIS_CLEANUP, but it had minimal effect. Additionally, the transaction RSDTIS_LIST_REQUEST does not appear to be available in our system, so we are unable to repair the DTIS table using that method.

entry.title: Re: Develop and deploy a HTML (Angular/Vue/React) app on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry) access S/4 On-premi

It would be great if you can share the repository.

I am trying to follow your document and trying to build an angular app in the same project where my CAP service exist in /srv module but your step 4 in this document is not clear hence I am a bit lost.

How can I achieve this? Please help.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Implementing direct links to dashboard activities via...

entry.title: Service PO and SES not created-Monitor accruals posting app
entry.content:
Hello,

We are on S/4 HANA Public cloud TM 2502 edition. I created advanced inter company  STO and the FU and  FO created and charges calculated in FO. 

CHarges calculation status= calculated in FO

Posting status in FO= Not ready for posting. 

No warehouse involved. Picking done manually in replenishment  outbound delivery but PGI not done.

execution status in FO= checked in and checked out at source location

 In Monitor Accruals posting app, the FO is not shown. Any idea what could be missing ?

All events below at source location of FO and also Set to "arrived/checked in" at destination location too but still FO is "not ready for posting "

Nothing shown in "Monitor accrual posting" app

 

rgds

sar

entry.title: Re: Configuration of Multiple VAT Rates in Sales Orders i...
entry.content: Thank you very much for your response, now I can start from that in my research.

entry.title: Re: What is using logical memory in my ASE server?
entry.content:
Re: interested in that large Global Block Pool as I've seen that growing in conjunction with total physical memory

  • total logical memory = 11727809 2k pages (~22.4 gbytes)
  • max memory = 12900883 2k pages (~26.6 gbytes)
  • allocate max shared memory was not set which is a whoops for us (normally it's set).  I have just set it/enabled it (set to 1)
  • total physical memory = 12900884 2k pages (~26.6 gbytes)

So the data cache is set to 7000 mbytes, but actual memory used (total logical memory) is up around 22.4 gbytes.   This is not urgent.  We have enough memory on the box, but it's curious

entry.title: Re: Skills Management between SAP S4HANA Service & SAP FSM
entry.content:

Also, is it possible to implement the integration using a customized iFlow?

Looking forward to your feedback.
Best regards,

entry.title: How to generate a hierarchy based data from flat data in cap java using TopLevels in OData
entry.content:
Hello there,

I am new in SAP CAP Java, i am looking for transforming a flat table data into a hierarchy based data using OData.

Let's take an example, let's assume this is the flat table data:



and  response should be:

I have created response exactly like the above response by customized tree based on the values from the flat table data. I want to implement this by using 'com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1.TopLevels'. I don't see any blogs ,Tutorials about this. do we have anything like that? or is it possible to do it?, Can anyone help me out? 

Thanks in advance!
Irbaaz

I am interested on how BOI Save Sequence errors are handled during Save Sequence and populated to User.

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content: I don't think we got into this, but if you have an iso_1 database you shouldn't really have multibyte characters in it. You should have the database changed to what characters are supported and can be converted in and out based on the client's request. This is usually set by the environment variables LC_ALL or LANG. There are connection properties based on the client settings like charset=xxx. Example: $ pwd /my/sybase/locales $ echo $LANG en_US.UTF-8 $ grep "en_US.UTF-8" * locales.dat: locale = en_US.UTF-8, us_english, utf8 My client would request utf8 charset and my database is iso_1 by default. There will be a conversion between client and ASE because they are not the same. Encoding or code page is different. That is more display or how the app handles the characters/hex. On windows you have chcp or "change code page" which displays the characters differently in the cmd prompt or window or app. Just make sure you can display the character and the hex in the database is what you want. select convert(columnname, binary) from tablename Compare the output with the hex that it should be. Hope that helps, Ryan

entry.title: Re: O que esperar no SAP Inside Track Salvador? #SITSAL2025
entry.content: Great @Marssel700

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Hello Sandra_Rossi,

I redid the CU50 in the background and there is still the DUMP problem. I see that there are frontend methods.
No problem, I activated 2 enhancements: 1 to put sy-batch = 'X' and the other to put sy-batch = space.
I get my hierarchical BOM back.

As for my 2nd table, I can't get my values since I have to press an additional button to display it. However, this last display does not exist in my Batch Input.
I found that the data creation was in the following location: 
SAPLKKBE - LKKBEF10
Table: AGT_T_KKB_SPLIT
But I can't deviate the program to go through this place so that I can retrieve this data.

If you have an idea to help me.
Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: ACADEMIC LESSONS: Your roadmap to teaching with SAP Learning Hub, student edition in the classro
entry.content:
laroussi_merhbene34

Please keep me posted ... I hope that the support team was able to help.  Some parts of the world had an extended holiday weekend so your support ticket may be in the queue.  If you do not get resolution by Tuesday please contact me via email:  universityalliances@SAP.com.  I will collect further details (like your email address, university you teach at, etc) directly instead of a public chat and see what we can do to resolve.

entry.title: Re: Separate line item for each invoice in payment document
entry.content: how to make separate customer payment line as per the invoice customer line in sap ecc

entry.title: Re: Bring Your Own Identity: SAP's Strategic Transformation of Identity and Authentication Managemen
entry.content: Well, Identity Renovation is an internal project name, and its all about enabling to connect your corporate IdP for logging into SAP ecosystem in future. This is meant by Bring-your-own-identity. There is a lot things mentioned in the video, also authentication. But thanks for the feedback, which shows us that you obviously expected to directly go into details. The reality outside is a bit different though, especially these technical topics most times need to be well explained upfront so many readers also understand on whats the point. We‘ll try to make a best fit all approach with our communications. Again thanks for the feedback.

entry.title: Re: Email Trigger Issue with OAuth2Password Authenticatio...
entry.content: Have you seen the note in https://sap.github.io/cloud-sdk/docs/js/v3/features/mail-client?

entry.title: How to Add Custom Fields from Custom CDS View into OData based App Variant (PO Goods Receipt)?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm working on a Fiori Elements app variant (s2p.mm.im.goodsreceipt.purchaseorder) 

I'm using SAP Business Application Studio.

Requirement is to add some custom fields in the item table as new columns. I was able to add the column details and also put some placeholders. But I'm stuck on how to map the field values.

I have created a new CDS view with the required fields and then published it via the OData Annotation, also checked its functionality in the transaction- /IWFND/GW_CLIENT.

I have then imported this OData to the Adaptation project using the Add OData and UI5 Model Wizard provided. I could see the change also recorded. Now, I have the following questions.

1. How can I check if the OData is properly linked with the project or not?
2. How can I map the fields which are part of this new OData at an item level for each PO?

I'm working on UI for the first time. Any comment would be of great help.
Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: TransportManagementService destination failing with 401
entry.content:
Ok i was able to fix this, i had to manually re-create the entire destination, every time you edit the destination the client ID value seems different, so to be consistent, anytime you make any change to the destination, "even if you click edit and save" with no changes  ..points to remember

1. Enter the client ID - again - copy from Service Key JSON.

2. Enter the client Secret value - again - copy from Service Key JSON. 

3. Token Service User and Token Service Password are NOT required, even if they are entered it will NOT be used until the "Use mTLS for token retrieval" is checked. 

Hope this helps. 

Regards

Ravi

entry.title: Re: How to Enable Editing of a Specific Field in AIF Monitoring and Error Handling
entry.content: @SpyrosSpyropoulos check the code in Function Module /AIF/AUTH_CHECK_CUSTOMIZING and as per above screenshot, it's locked for any editing.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/331648

But I have no inforation when such solution will be available. The only thing that comes to me is creating an extnsion to cover your requirements.

Best regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Production order GR .Document XXXXXXX does not contain any selectable items Message No M7064
entry.content:
Getting an error while doing Goods receipt (MIGO) for a Production order.  Production operations are completed (CNF), all operation are confirmed with total yield qty, it was never deleted or teco'd, doesn't have any COGI either, planning plant and production plant is same, I Don't see anything wrong with it , but still getting that error, could you please help? Below is the Status's for Production order. 
REL Released
PRT Printed
CNF Confirmed
PRC Pre-costed
BCRQ Order to be handled in batches
MACM Material committed
RESA Results analysis carried out
SETC Settlement rule created. 

Thank you.

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: What is New for SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals?
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to share what is new for SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals as part of the 1H 2025 product release. We are unveiling a modern and refreshed performance form experience designed with your feedback in mind, new AI capabilities, and enhancements to continuous performance management capabilities. All built to help you focus on what matters most—your people.

We are excited to unveil the new performance form experience with a new interface and several AI-assisted capabilities designed to improve efficiency and support more consistent, high-quality evaluations. The layout has been updated to be more responsive and easier to navigate. Information panels now appear only when needed, providing context—such as historical comments or rating guidance—without crowding the screen. The result is a cleaner, more task-focused interface that reduces unnecessary scrolling and helps users stay focused while completing reviews.


Enhanced performance form experience

This redesigned experience also includes AI-assisted comments feature, which suggests well-crafted, insightful starter comments that align with the ratings provided on skills, goals, and competencies. These serve as a starting point and helps evaluators provide constructive, growth-centric feedback, reducing the effort required to write detailed feedback, especially in large-scale reviews. That is not all. This experience also offers AI-assisted feedback in performance reviews, which helps refine and improve communications by integrating rating context and user input to make performance form feedback more relevant and precise.


AI-assisted comments feature


AI-assisted feedback in performance reviews

For managers, this new experience provides AI-assisted performance insights. This feature analyzes a wealth of talent data from continuous feedback to achievements, to the mid-year performance assessment and summarizes the employee’s strengths, achievements, and areas for improvement. This feature gives managers a quick yet comprehensive understanding of an employee’s performance with actionable recommendations tailored to each employee's strengths and areas for improvement.


AI-assisted performance insights

With this release, we are also introducing AI-based sentiment analysis for 360-degree reviews to help employees quickly identify positive, negative or mixed feedback, highlighting areas that need improvement and enabling focused professional development. This makes it easier to spot areas that require attention or improvement without having to manually sift through each comment. It also helps highlight areas where the employee is consistently recognized, which can be useful for both motivation and development planning.


AI-based sentiment analysis for 360-degree reviews

And for continuous performance management, employees can now link activities and achievements across various goal plans, leveraging greater flexibility to reflect everyday efforts and evolving priorities across the organization. This helps ensure comprehensive alignment with organization’s priorities as they evolve throughout the year, especially when activities fall under different strategic goals.


Link achievements and activities to multiple goal plans feature

Want to See It in Action?

Check out this short demo video that describes the updated experience and highlights how these innovations and enhancements work in practice. You can also visit the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2025 Product Release page to download the full release brochure, overview video featuring Bianka Woelke, and more.

All product features and delivery dates mentioned in this document represent current product development expectations only and not a delivery commitment. You should base your purchasing, administrative, and configuration decisions solely on functionality that is currently available.

entry.title: Re: Can the job requisition approver catch the dynamic ro...
entry.content:
You cannot directly access those roles from EC. You can only fetch the roles defined on the position as part of the position matrix relationship, or fetch users based on certain data in EC, such as business unit, department, or the logged-in user.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
Does anybody have any good debugging tips for Document Compliance?  I just counted 8 SAP incidents we have logged with the product when I realized there is no way of debugging basic things like product connectivity.  For example, it took 3 days before Concur Invoice could "see" the BTP connection in Cloud Foundry. 

I've had what appears to be a Communication Arrangement error between S4HC and Doc Compliance BTP, but error is cryptic and debugging tools are nonexistent - so where do your start?  The case management Document Compliance Support team is invisible.  Proxy error SOAP:1007 SRT: Unsupported xstream found: ("HTTP C: ode 401 : Unauthorized") is an example where you follow  Communication Arrangements steps but when it doesn't work - who do you call? Hey Ghostbusters - the flux capacitor isn't working. What do i do?

entry.title: Re: How to Add Custom Fields from Custom CDS View into OD...
entry.content: you have to enhance original service, creating your own odata service won't help.

entry.title: Backflush Components Not Appearing in Pick List Form
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I've noticed that components set to backflush are not appearing in the Pick List form.
Is this the expected behavior, or could there be a configuration I'm missing?

Thanks in advance for your insights!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Backflush Components Not Appearing in Pick List Form
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, this is the standard system behavior for the Pick List form - components set to backflush are not displayed.

As a workaround, you can refer to the following note:

  • 2867839 - Output Management: Pick List contains too many components.

According to the note, it's possible to create a custom form template using the Data List Service FDP_PP_MOR_DATA_LIST_SRV.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Re: What is using logical memory in my ASE server?
entry.content:
I'm running ASE 16.0 SP04 PL04 on a home (linux) server.

My total logical memory matches up (within a couple % points) with the totals from monMemoryUsage + total data cache.

If I were seeing this kind of discrepancy (while waiting for feedback from tech support?) I'd start looking for data that may not be showing up in monMemoryUsage, eg:

  • review entire output from sp_configure looking for outsized total memory usage numbers
  • review sp_configure Monitoring for some outsized MDA pipe max messages settings
  • review output from sp_monitorconfig 'all' looking for outsized Max_Used values that could be eating up memory
  • review the list of MDA tables; objective being to run queries against MDA tables that contain memory usage numbers; perhaps there are some items (eg, rep thread memory usage?) that are not accounted for in monMemoryUsage
  • rule out connection-related issues by bouncing the ASE instance and bring up on a new/different port (that applications don't know about) and make sure all repagents are shutdown; primary objective is to see if total logical memory is in line with expected usage and if it is then look at monitoring user/application connections and repagent activity
  • do other ASE instances show this same discrepancy in total logical memory? if 'no', what's different between the ASE instances?

I'm assuming sp_configure memory is reporting (at the end) that ~4.2 GB of memory is available for use, right?

entry.title: Power Platform help for pushing data in SAP - "IN CASE OF ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT, PLEASE ENTER ACC. ASSI
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using power automate to create a  flow so that I can push the data from my power app to the SAP.

I am creating PO using power app and in which everything is working proper but I have faced an issued regarding POACCOUNT parameter.
Error is such that I have written all the required fields in it and also in the POACCOUNTX parameter I have flagged the fields but still while running the flow it is not showing the data of the POACCOUNT parameter.

It is showing error "IN CASE OF ACCOUNT ASSIGNMENT, PLEASE ENTER ACC. ASSIGNMENT DATA FOR ITEM" even after mentioning every necessary this . Is something that I am missing the POACCOUNT parameter is not even showing a single field in it (POACCOUNT[]).

 

Please guide me regarding this error.

SAP S/4HANA for Microsoft Teams SAP Integrated Product Development

entry.title: Re: Organizational level of supplier evaluation in SAP S/...
entry.content:
Hello, 

There is an app Overall Supplier Evaluation that shows KPI regarding supplier evaluation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/97954958371c9144e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

You can set here filters default for user, to see evaluated suppliers on required organisation level.

As for different criteria in each Company Code, you can create a Template specifically for each Company requirement and users will be able to choose the one that interests them. Here you will find whole documentation regarding use of Supplier Evaluation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/178e7dae5abe4d4aaa55f434aba4f703.html?locale=en-US

And here regarding supplier evaluation results:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/99edf736476e46c397f2529886750117.html?locale=en-US

Best regards, 

Jagoda Drwiega

entry.title: Preferred unit of measure
entry.content:
Hi,

Please clarify how preferred units of measure is intended to work in Public cloud?

Unit of measure conversion

Preferred unit of measure BAG.

1.) Is it possible to maintain pref.unit of measure in warehouse level?

2.) If PO is created in 48pcs -> inbound 48 pcs -> why system creates task as 1 PAL?

3.) In outbound scenario, is pref. unit of measure supposed to be the unit in picking task?

In customizing the stock.specific unit of measure is BAG only.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
The call transaction does not allow you to take into account the buttons I asked for on the last screen.

Here is my code:

PERFORM bdc_dynpro USING 'SAPLCKDI' '4610'.
PERFORM bdc_field USING 'BDC_OKCODE' '/00'.
PERFORM bdc_field USING 'BDC_CURSOR' 'CKI64A-KLVAR'.
PERFORM bdc_field USING 'CKI64A-KLVAR' ws_costing-klvar.
PERFORM bdc_field USING 'BDC_OKCODE' '=KEPH'.

ws_opt-nobinpt = 'X'.
ws_opt-dismode = 'N'.
ws_opt-updmode = 'A'.

CALL TRANSACTION 'CU50' USING bdcdata
OPTIONS FROM ws_opt
INTO wt_messtab.

I tried to modify the dismode option with different values but the OK_CODE =KEPH is not taken into account.

Please let me know if anyone has implemented SM30 functionality using RAP?

Thanks,

BK

entry.title: Re: How can I make non-key field columns editable on Fior...
entry.content: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-business-configuration-object..html

entry.title: Text for field IOHEADER-GSTRI (Actual start time) wrong in PP Orders - S/4 HANA 2023
entry.content:
Problems

Text for field IOHEADER-GSTRI (Actual start time) wrong in Englis/Spanish. Report standard COOIS – Header. Example in Systems.

 

The solution is to apply the notes:

A.- 155376 - CO26-28: Text for field IOHEADER-GSTRI wrong
The text for field IOHEADER-GSTRI is 'Actual start time' instead of 'Actual start date' in the field selection dialog.
Solution
Change the text for field IOHEADER-GSTRI as follows:
1. Start report RCOTX000 in transaction SE38.
2. Enter IOHEADER as the field string name, maintenance language E
(for English), order category 10
3. Execute (F8)
4. Scroll to the line, where Field is GSTRI
5. Change the text 'Actual start time' in column eight (from the left)
to 'Actual start date'
6. Save the changes

If the note for item A does not work, apply the note:

B.- 2568682 - COOIS: Field 'Actual start time' is duplicate - follow up of note 520429.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content: Its a very exciting time to be in Payroll, thanks for the great overview.

entry.title: Re: how to set Granted user's company as a target population
entry.content:
Hi,

Just create one role.

Create 10 different granted population assign each administrator on the group

Create 10 different target population as per your requirement. 

now, on the role, create a new assignment by clicking "add role assignment", with the desired granted and population on it, here you can configure in such a way, that HRBP's from USA can only see the employees from the country USA or Employee's belongs to the company codes in USA. 

Create assignment, on the define a target population section, select the radio button of "Filtered by" enable "permission group" here you will get the select the target population and then save.

Thanks. 

Nisha

entry.title: oninit rule not triggered after employee import
entry.content:
Hello community,

We've configured to autopoulate the value "5" for "working days per week' field, this oninit rule successfully works in add new employee, but the value does not autopopulate using import employee data

entry.title: Access Performance Forms Directly from SAP SuccessFactors Stories: New in 1H 2025!
entry.content:
Overview

A highly anticipated enhancement is now available in SAP SuccessFactors Stories as part of the 1H 2025 release, offering the ability to open Performance Management forms directly from a Stories, in Print Preview format.

With just a click on the Document ID, users can jump straight to the PM form without being part of the route map.

Let’s explore how this works and walk through the step-by-step configuration so anyone (even first-time Story designers) can build this into their reports.

Why Is This Feature Important?

  • View the Performance Management form from within a report

  • If you have access to PM form data in Stories, the direct deeplink can be configured in Stories, providing you access to PM form
  • No need to navigate to other modules or be in the performance management workflow
  • Based on a Customer Influence Idea - it’s built from customer feedback!

What You’ll Need

Before you begin, make sure:

  • You have access to Stories in Report Center.

  • You have the data permission: User > Performance > Data Access: Story

Step-by-Step Guide: Configure Hyperlink to PM Forms

Step 1: Create a New Story Report

  1. Go to Report Center > Create > Story

  2. Choose Optimized Design Experience
  3. Click Create

Step 2: Select the Data Connection

  1. In the popup, choose:

    • SAP Live Connection

    • Select SAPSFSFREP

  2. Click OK

Step 3: Use Query Designer

  1. In Query Designer, From Available Schemas:

    • Expand the schema Performance Management

    • Select a Performance form template

  2. Drag towards the left to add Form Template and Form, then select fields like Document ID and Form Template Name or other relevant fields in your report.

  3. Important: Ensure Document ID is added as a Dimension

    • Click the Column Overview icon

    • Confirm it appears under Dimension, not Measure, as shown in the snapshot below.
    • Click on Preview Query, verify sample data output, and click Finish so the system navigates you to Story Designer.

Step 4: Add a Table to the Story

  1. In the Story Designer, from the menu bar or the left pane, add a Table Widget.

  2. From the Builder Panel, under Table Structure, Choose Cross Tab. If the Builder panel is not visible, select the right side panel from Menu bar.
  3. From the Builder Panel again, add Dimensions: Document ID, Form Template Name.

Step 5: Add Hyperlink to Document ID

  1. Click on the Document ID column, click the 3-dot (More Actions) menu on the right.

  2. Click + Add > Hyperlink

Configure the hyperlink:

  • Link To: External URL

  • External URL: Enter the below 
    https://<your-SuccessFactors-domain>/sf/printPreviewPMForm?fDataId=Document ID
     
    Example:
    https://hcm2preview.sapsf.eu/sf/printPreviewPMForm?fDataId=Document ID

Pro Tip: Copy the domain directly from your logged-in browser tab.

  • Expand IN USE

  • Select Document ID (don’t type manually)
  • Check Open in new tab
  • Ensure the URL configuration appears as shown in snapshot below
  • Click Done

Step 6: Enable the Hyperlinks in the Table

  1. Click the top left of the table widget

  2. Choose Navigate to External URL
  3. Click to enable “Show Hyperlinks in Table”

Now, each Document ID will be a clickable link that opens the employee’s PM form in Print Preview mode.

You're Done!

With just a few simple steps, you've enabled seamless, direct access to Performance forms within Stories, enhancing usability, speeding up reviews, and reducing admin overhead.

This enhancement is a big win for HR teams, managers, and power users who rely on Performance data for decision-making.

Final Tips

  • Test the hyperlink in View mode to ensure it's working as expected

  • You can also create filters or add employee-specific info to personalize the report

Let’s Keep the Ideas Coming!

This feature was delivered via Customer Influence, a powerful way to shape SAP's roadmap. Don’t forget to vote, comment, and share your ideas in the Customer Influence Portal.

And if this guide helped you, leave a like, drop a comment, or hit that Kudos button!

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals  SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics #1H2025Release

entry.title: Re: Whitholding Tax Configuration in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud P...
entry.content: Yes

entry.title: Set Document Class
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I'm trying to set document type C1 in transaction F-28, but it doesn't appear. In the User Parameters tab (SU01), I added the BAR parameter, and whenever I access the F-28 TRX, it shows me document type DZ.

Is there another way to set the document type?

Thank you.

#FI

entry.title: Activity type Assignment is not available in FSM mobile App
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am currently working on creating activities via the SAP FSM mobile application. While I am able to view and create different activity types such as Tasks, Meetings, etc., these activities do not appear on the dispatching board.

After comparing the activities created through the web by the dispatcher or the ones replicated for the ERP, I noticed that those displayed on the dispatching board are of type Assignment, which unfortunately is not available as an option on the mobile app.

Could you kindly advise on how to address this? The objective is to enable technicians to independently report activities related to a service order from the mobile app, with these activities then replicated as new items in SAP S/4HANA Service (Private Edition).

Your support in resolving this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: In S4 HANA Public Cloud where do I find Configuration...
entry.content:
Hi Dan,

Thank you for your question.

“Define Versions” leads to MRP and not Costing in your S/4HANA Public Cloud because the search term corresponds to a more generic SSCUI for planning versions used in MRP. To access costing versions, use SSCUI 102617 or ensure you are in the Product Cost Planning configuration area with scope item BJE activated.

 

Checkpoint

 What to Do

Incorrect Role Assigned

Ensure the role SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT (Business Process Configuration Expert) is assigned to your user.

Scope Item Not Activated

Confirm that Scope Item BJE – Product Cost Planning is activated in your system.

Search Too Broad

Instead of searching “Define Versions”, use the specific SSCUI number: 102617 to go directly to the correct configuration.

App Filter Too Narrow

Expand your filters by selecting All Application Areas, or specifically Finance, to view relevant costing-related apps.

Just let me know if it works

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: IDEA integration for Turkey Incoming invoices
entry.content:
Hi,

you may consider contacting on eof the service providers. here is one withe their information in the SAP Store:

https://store.sap.com/dcp/en/product/display-2002001998_live_v1/sni-turkey-e-invoice-certified-last-mile-partner-for-sap-document-and-reporting-compliance

Best regards,

Martin

entry.title: Management for Deprecated or Decommissioned CDS Views with an upgrade
entry.content:
We have upgraded our Sandbox environment from 1909 to 2023 and noticed that a few CDS views are no longer available by default and are deleted as of SAP S/4HANA 2023 release. What is the process to identify which CDS views are deprecated or replacing any deprecated CDS views with the successor? With each software upgrade, we understand there will be occurrences of deprecated CDS views or deprecated CDS view elements in the custom content, How do we find out which are deprecated or how do we utilize the Extensibility Inventory app to identify the deprecation? Is there way we can get a list or a link for SAP HELP to identify the deprecated CDS views in advance as these are out of the box CDS views that are being consumed in our environment?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Management for Deprecated or Decommissioned CDS Views...
entry.content:
In order to manage upcoming deprecation of CDS views, please review the following documentation on the SAP Help Portal:

Learning About Deprecated CDS Views | SAP Help Portal

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Create Service Order by technicians from SAP FSM and replicate it to SAP S4HANA Service
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope you are doing well.

We are currently implementing SAP Field Service Management integrated with SAP S/4HANA Service (Private Edition) using SAP CPI.

As part of the project scope, we have a scenario where technicians should be able to report new issues directly from the field — meaning without an existing service order.

Could you please advise on the optimal way to handle this scenario?
Would it be possible to create a new service order directly from the SAP FSM mobile application?

The intended process is to create a service order from the mobile app, linked to the relevant equipment, and replicate it to SAP S/4HANA Service. From there, the service administrator would assign the appropriate service item and Spare parts before sending it back to FSM for planning and execution.

I would appreciate your guidance on how best to implement this process.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: In S4 HANA Public Cloud where do I find Configuration...
entry.content:
Hello Chuma,

Thanks again but this isn't the issue either.  ID 102617 takes me to Profitability Area.

entry.title: Re: how shipping pt. get capture in OBD via transfer posting
entry.content:
Please check SAP KBA 3067316 - Shipping Point in delivery created from post goods movement (MIGO) - SAP for Me

Also, check what you have configured under Logistics Execution-->Decentralized WMS Integration-->Inventory Management Interface and Delivery-Relevant Data-->Define Default Values for Deliveries

entry.title: BSEG: How to identify Transfer Variant used
entry.content:
Hello All,

Using ABT1N I have posted intercompany asset. In Additional Details Tab, we have transfer varaint.

How to identify in BSEG, for the accounting doc posted using ABT1N which transfer variant was used. I am not able to identify that. 

Can you please help me?
